title: BLOCK 2 FINAL: Punctuation mastery
description: Closing test for Block 2 — all punctuation marks at 30 WPM
text: she wrote: "the path was long, but worth it; we learned much, and grew strong." he replied: "that's the key — patience pays off!" they shook hands; the year ended well.

tips.text: The closing test for the block, and every mark you have learned is in it. Colons, semicolons, quotation marks, the lot. Set it down like finished copy.
Clear this at thirty words a minute with fewer than fourteen slips and the block is behind you. Pace, not panic, gets you there.

tips.target: she wrote: "the path was long, but worth it; we learned much, and grew strong."

tips.text: Past this gate the numbers and symbols open up. New keys, same calm hand. Finish this first.

tips.target: he replied: "that's the key — patience pays off!" they shook hands; the year ended well.

character_tips.anna: This test closes the block. The punctuation is yours now. Numbers and symbols are the next stretch of road. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: The marks are mastered. The next block brings the number row and the symbols above it, and a few new reaches to learn. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: One clean passage and the block is done. Hold your tempo through the marks and walk it through. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: You have the punctuation in hand now. The next adventure is numbers, and you are ready for it. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
